description: Blend ITSM incidents from ServiceNow/Jira with CrowdStrike Falcon detections and assets to understand operational and cyber risk at the service and CI level.

raw_schemas.table_ddl:
-- ServiceNow Incident table (incident)
CREATE TABLE sn_incident (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  number STRING,
  short_description STRING,
  description STRING,
  category STRING,
  subcategory STRING,
  priority STRING,
  impact STRING,
  urgency STRING,
  state STRING,
  opened_at TIMESTAMP,
  resolved_at TIMESTAMP,
  closed_at TIMESTAMP,
  caller_id STRING,
  assignment_group STRING,
  assigned_to STRING,
  u_business_service STRING,
  cmdb_ci STRING,
  company STRING,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- ServiceNow CMDB CI (cmdb_ci)
CREATE TABLE sn_cmdb_ci (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  name STRING,
  sys_class_name STRING,
  fqdn STRING,
  ip_address STRING,
  install_status STRING,
  u_business_service STRING,
  environment STRING,
  owned_by STRING,
  company STRING,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- Jira Service Management issues
CREATE TABLE jira_itsm_issue (
  id STRING NOT NULL,
  issue_key STRING,
  project_key STRING,
  issuetype_name STRING,
  summary STRING,
  description STRING,
  priority_name STRING,
  status_name STRING,
  created TIMESTAMP,
  updated TIMESTAMP,
  resolutiondate TIMESTAMP,
  reporter STRING,
  assignee STRING,
  customfield_service STRING,
  customfield_ci STRING,
  customfield_request_type STRING
);

-- Jira Assets / CMDB objects
CREATE TABLE jira_cmdb_ci (
  object_id STRING NOT NULL,
  object_key STRING,
  object_name STRING,
  object_type STRING,
  hostname STRING,
  ip_address STRING,
  environment STRING,
  business_service_key STRING,
  is_critical BOOLEAN,
  updated TIMESTAMP
);

-- CrowdStrike Falcon detections (simplified)
CREATE TABLE cs_falcon_detection (
  detection_id STRING NOT NULL,
  customer_id STRING,
  device_id STRING,
  host_name STRING,
  local_ip STRING,
  tactic STRING,
  technique STRING,
  severity STRING,
  status STRING,
  platform_name STRING,
  created_timestamp TIMESTAMP,
  first_behavior_timestamp TIMESTAMP,
  last_behavior_timestamp TIMESTAMP
);

-- CrowdStrike Falcon devices
CREATE TABLE cs_falcon_device (
  device_id STRING NOT NULL,
  hostname STRING,
  local_ip STRING,
  os_version STRING,
  platform_name STRING,
  site_name STRING,
  asset_criticality STRING,
  first_seen TIMESTAMP,
  last_seen TIMESTAMP
);

-- Unified business service dimension
CREATE TABLE business_services (
  service_id STRING NOT NULL,
  source_system STRING,
  source_service_key STRING,
  service_name STRING,
  service_tier STRING,
  business_unit STRING,
  env STRING,
  criticality INTEGER,
  owner_group STRING,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP
);

lod_configurations.description: One row per Jira ITSM issue

user_query: Create a risk and operational dashboard that correlates ServiceNow and Jira incidents with CrowdStrike Falcon detections and devices, rolled up to business services and CIs.
data_mart_goals: Link ITSM incidents from ServiceNow and Jira to Falcon devices and detections via hostname/IP to understand which services are under active attack.
Compute combined operational and security health scores per business service (incidents, MTTR, detection volume, detection severity).
Provide trends for detections, incidents, and service health by environment and business unit.
metric_questions: Which business services have both high incident volume and high Falcon detection activity?
For each service, what is the count of high-severity detections vs P1/P2 incidents over the last 90 days?
Which CIs/devices have repeated Falcon detections and repeated ITSM incidents?
How does MTTR for security-related incidents compare to other incident types?
What is the composite risk score per business service when combining incident MTTR, incident volume, and detection severity mix?
